title: CSS Architecture Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating robust and cross-browser web experiences with architecture and code structure.

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a professional-grade web application in CSS?
options: Table Layouts
Float Layouts
Using a consistent naming convention like BEM (Block Element Modifier) to create explicit, independent CSS rules.
Absolute Positioning only

questions.question: Which of these is a major 'Pro' of using CSS architecture (like BEM, OOCSS, or SMACSS) compared to raw CSS?
options: It makes your CSS files smaller and more readable.
It helps developers organize their code into a structured, maintainable, and highly reusable design system.
It is always supported by every browser.
It replaces the need for any HTML5 tags.

questions.question: What is 'Atomic Design' inside a professional web application?
options: To make the site load much faster.
To break the UI down into five levels: Atoms (buttons), Molecules (search bar), Organisms (navbar), Templates, and Pages.
To choose the colors for the animation.
To automatically generate CSS animations.

questions.question: Why is it important to almost ALWAYS avoid 'Universal Selectors' (*) everywhere in your professional CSS architecture?
options: They are difficult to maintain.
They can make the site load much slower by being too broad and potentially conflicting with other styles.
They are not supported in modern browsers.
They take up no space in the browser.

questions.question: Which of these is a best practice for a professional-style stylesheet for architecture?
options: Commenting every single line of code.
Using a reset or normalize stylesheet.
Splitting styles into a modular folder structure (base, components, layout, pages).
Using variables for colors and spacing.
